free_text: Alpha-cluster structure of 18Ne
Experimment performed at the
Cyclotron Institute at Texas AM University using the
active target TexAT
Active target TexAT filled with the gas mixture 96 %
of 4He and 4 % of CO2. The gas pressure was 580 Torr.
TexAT consists of a gas-filled Time Projection
Chamber and two layers of solid states detectors
which surround the TPC in Cartesian geometry. TPCs
has a high level of segmentation in the readout plane
and provide 3D-vertex and tracking capabilities for
incoming beam ions and charged reaction products. The
gas pressure of 580 Torr was chosen to stop the 14O
beam before the last 1/8th of the active volume. This
last portion of the active volume was used as a dE
detector to identify the alpha particles hitting the
silicon wall.
Inverse kinematics was used
The 14-O beam was produced with the reaction 14N(p,n)
14-O using magnetic achromat recoil separator at the
Cyclotron Institute at Texas AM University. The 14N
primary beam with energy of 11 MeV/A was delivered by
the K500 Cyclotron and directed into a 9.2-cm-long
liquid-nitrogen-cooled gas cell. The energy of the
14-O beam was 61.8 MeV with an intensity of about
10E+4 pps.
Origin of uncertainties not described
Compiled by S.H.
Data presented in fig. 5 of the reference
sent by author M.B.

this work
